commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: And the four and twenty elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God that sat on the throne, saying, Amen; Alleluia. Amen; Alleluia: Although these words can be interpreted, for they are translated, as I said, "to the faith," or "truth," and "praise of the Lord," yet in reverence for their sacredness the authority of the original language is preserved to them. For the Church also continually sings Alleluia on the Lord's day, and the whole period of the fifty days, because of the hope of the resurrection, which is to be in praise of the Lord.

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: worshipped: The Church worships God, not with the service of the lips alone, but with the sweetness of the highest devotion.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: But in Leviticus He says: "Go not ye after idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: I am the Lord your God."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
